complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about stroke, MI, aortic dissection, VTE, meningitis/encephalitis, sepsis, spinal cord injury/compression, lung cancer
process_step: first clinician assessment; imaging/lab interpretation; discharge
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Systematic review estimated diagnostic error harms and common high-harm conditions.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Missed/delayed: serious ED diagnoses, especially vascular, neurologic, infection and cancer presentations.
outcome_if_known: varies
product_or_system_implication: Flag high-harm conditions linked to symptom clusters and require explicit exclusion/safety-netting before discharge.
notes: Useful as cross-setting failure evidence rather than a local workflow manual.

review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: “I was constantly dismissed, ignored and belittled”
complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about endometriosis
process_step: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Survey documented repeated GP visits and A&E attendance before diagnosis; respondents reported dismissal/minimisation.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Missed/delayed: endometriosis diagnosis; patients often required repeated self-advocacy.
outcome_if_known: average 8 years 10 months
product_or_system_implication: Track symptom duration/severity, repeated visits and invalidating language; prompt specialist referral criteria review.
notes: Advocacy survey gives patient voice and quantitative delay data.

review_or_complaint_excerpt_short: “I really tried to get the doctors to listen”
complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about cancer and other scan-detectable conditions
process_step: imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; emergency return
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Ombudsman reported repeated failures in scan reading, carrying out scans and follow-up; a cancer case was only identified at a fifth A&E visit after lesion not reported.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Missed/delayed: abnormal scan findings and cancer diagnosis.
outcome_if_known: example six-week delay; other cases vary
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop imaging: critical finding acknowledgment, patient notification and escalation if no action documented.
notes: Patient-complaint/ombudsman evidence with public family quote.

setting_or_context: community health center / public health screening
complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about colorectal cancer risk after abnormal FIT
process_step: public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Mailed FIT outreach identified abnormal results; centralized patient navigation addressed informational, emotional, financial and transport barriers to colonoscopy.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Identified: abnormal FIT needing colonoscopy; missed: patients not reached or declining navigation remain at risk.
outcome_if_known: follow-up within 9 months measured
product_or_system_implication: Track abnormal-screen closure and navigator barrier categories to completion.
notes: Implementation study in FQHC safety-net setting; strong fit to product workflows.

complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about abnormal FIT requiring colonoscopy
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: PRECISE trial implementation support trained navigators and documented challenges in workload, documentation, partnerships and tracking.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Identified: implementation barriers to closing abnormal FIT follow-up.
outcome_if_known: varies
product_or_system_implication: Build navigator work queues, cross-organization data sharing and colonoscopy-status reconciliation.
notes: Recent implementation lessons for community health settings.

complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about urgent/emergency cases among general OPD patients
process_step: arrival; nurse triage; vitals; first clinician assessment
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Rural OPD implemented IITT-adapted structured triage using a three-colour checklist on charts and short training; patient flow improved without added resources.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Identified: urgent/emergency cases earlier; missed: undertriage still possible and formal triage requires staff buy-in.
outcome_if_known: time to provider reduced 23 minutes; total OPD stay reduced 35 minutes
product_or_system_implication: Low-cost triage checklists can be embedded into paper charts and later digitized.
notes: Real-world QI study in rural Uganda.

complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about non-specific symptoms and signs of cancer
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Cohort study of urgent referral to Denmark's non-organ-specific cancer pathway; around one fifth had cancer.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Identified: substantial cancer prevalence among non-specific symptom referrals; missed: patients with serious symptoms outside pathway criteria.
outcome_if_known: 2014-2021 cohort
product_or_system_implication: Support structured referral for uncertainty, not just classic red flags.
notes: Peer-reviewed evidence for nonspecific symptom diagnostic pathways.

complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about rare congenital/neurologic condition
process_step: repeat visit; specialist review; imaging/genetic/special testing
what_person_wanted: recognition, follow-up, or escalation
what_service_or_staff_did: Patient story describes self-advocacy leading to specialist programme review and eventual rare diagnosis after decades.
research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
what_was_missed_or_failed: Missed/delayed: rare diagnosis across many earlier encounters.
outcome_if_known: about 30 years
product_or_system_implication: Give patients a longitudinal symptom/problem timeline they can share across systems.
notes: Patient story, useful for unusual-flow capture; low generalizability.

notes: Patient-story source; useful qualitative signal, not a systematic estimate.

complaint_context: Public complaint/experience source about varied common diseases with potential harm

notes: Peer-reviewed diagnostic-error study; old but foundational.
